commands.guard: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 -m compileall -q hipengine tests scripts && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 pytest -q tests/test_generation_batch_scheduler.py tests/test_generation_qwen35_paro.py tests/test_qwen35_resident_batch_layout.py tests/test_kvcache_policy.py tests/test_kvcache_spans.py tests/test_server_api.py -q && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_correctness.py --rows 2 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-primitive-c2.json && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_correctness.py --rows 8 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-primitive-c8.json

conclusion: Native-batch-c1 oracle resolves the c4 comparator ambiguity. Rowchunk2 matches native-batch c1 exactly for the short early window, while full-native has immediate hidden drift at layer4/7 (tokens still green for only 8 decode steps). The c4 full-native blocker is therefore real hidden drift in full-native grouping, not an artifact of the serial hidden oracle or retained sequence accounting.

prompt_verifier.evidence: PASS: native-batch-c1 hidden comparator makes the focused c4 blocker split green/red: rowchunk2 is hidden/token green over decode0-8, while full-native is hidden red immediately with layer4 and layer7 drift. This eliminates the serial-c1 oracle ambiguity and directly isolates the full-native attention/grouping blocker.
